evidence_statements.faithful_clinical_statement: SCADDING ET AL. | 859 F I G U R E 1 Immunological mechanisms of Allergic Rhinitis. Sensitized patients with allergic rhinitis have IgE antibodies for specific allergen(s) bound to receptors on the surface of mast cells. On re-exposure to the specific allergen(s), cross-linking of adjacent IgE molecules occurs, and mast cell degranulation results. Pre-formed mediators such as histamine stimulate sensory nerve endings within seconds, causing itch and sneezing, and promote dilatation of local vasculature and glandular secretion, causing obstruction and rhinorrhoea, respectively.
